key_insight: The paper proves hp-optimal discontinuous Galerkin approximation and develops robust additive Schwarz decompositions for variable-order finite element spaces on one-irregular cubical meshes.
engineering_relevance: This enables efficient, scalable solvers and error-controlled simulations for heterogeneous diffusion/interface problems on adaptive 3D hexahedral meshes.
method: hp interpolation on fitted conforming closures of one-irregular vertex patches
Interior penalty DG error analysis for fitted homogeneous diffusion interface problems
Stable additive Schwarz decompositions for conforming and DG spaces, with DG-to-conforming reduction for uniform-degree 3D cases
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical hp-optimal energy-norm estimates and robustness bounds, and notes numerical experiments validating the DG error estimate and Schwarz preconditioner performance.
watch_item: Robustness in the 3D conforming decomposition includes a polylogarithmic loss, and coefficient-contrast independence requires a local quasi-monotonicity condition.
paper_title: hp-Optimal DG Approximation and Robust Schwarz Decompositions on One-Irregular Cubical Meshes
